questions.question: What is the primary difference between guided and unguided media?
options: Guided media is faster and more reliable
Unguided media allows for more flexibility
Guided media is more secure than unguided media
Unguided media is used for wireless networks
questions.explanation: Unguided media, such as wireless, does not require a physical medium like a cable or fiber optic cable. Guided media, such as coaxial cables, use a physical guide to transmit data.

questions.question: Which layer of the OSI model is responsible for error-free transfer of data?
options: Physical Layer
Data Link Layer
Network Layer
Transport Layer
questions.explanation: The Physical Layer is responsible for error-free transfer of data. It deals with the physical aspects of data communication, such as transmission media and signal transmission.

questions.question: What is the primary function of the TCP/IP protocol suite?
options: To provide error-free data transfer
To facilitate communication between devices on the same network
To enable communication between devices on different networks
To provide a common language for devices to communicate
questions.explanation: The TCP/IP protocol suite enables communication between devices on different networks by providing a common language and a set of rules for data transfer.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of guided media?
options: Ethernet cable
Fiber optic cable
Wireless network
Satellite internet
questions.explanation: Ethernet cable and fiber optic cable are examples of guided media, as they use a physical guide to transmit data.

questions.question: What is the primary function of the Data Link Layer?
options: To provide error-free data transfer
To facilitate communication between devices on the same network
To enable communication between devices on different networks
To provide a common language for devices to communicate
questions.explanation: The Data Link Layer facilitates communication between devices on the same network by providing error-free data transfer and addressing devices on the network.
